purpose: Restore CyberShield's model library inside Architecture as the system-of-thinking layer, while Proof Pack remains the evidence-backed output layer.
boundary: Prototype model library only. These entries describe intended model behavior and demo/scaffold use. They do not perform live retrieval, live fact-checking, live scoring, statistical validation, backend persistence, or production enforcement.
architecture_rule: Architecture explains how CyberShield thinks. Proof Pack shows what CyberShield can defend later.

categories.label: Core Trust Models
categories.description: Models that explain trust posture, runtime authority, and executive decision state.

categories.label: Evidence Models
categories.description: Models that assess source quality, evidence completeness, freshness, relevance, and traceability.

categories.label: Internet Trust Engine
categories.description: Models for online artifacts, claims, sources, transcripts, wrapper metadata, and risk if believed.

categories.label: AI Governance Models
categories.description: Models for AI systems, agent authority, model provenance, tool use, and human review.

categories.label: Vendor and Supply Chain Models
categories.description: Models for third-party trust, contractual reliance, dependency concentration, and vendor proof.

categories.label: Decision and Consequence Models
categories.description: Models that connect trust state to business consequence, approval, exception, and next action.

categories.label: Proof Pack Models
categories.description: Models that turn decision records, evidence, assumptions, and model traces into defensible outputs.

models.name: Universal Model Trace Inspector
models.purpose: Inspect every score, model output, recommendation, map node, or trust state and explain what produced it.
models.boundary: Inspection scaffold only until connected to the full model registry and runtime evidence store.

models.name: Core Trust Score
models.purpose: Summarize whether the evaluated action is trusted, provisionally trusted, degraded, contested, or blocked.

models.name: Operational Trust Score
models.purpose: Evaluate whether current operating conditions support allowing, constraining, escalating, or blocking an action.

models.name: Evidence Trust Score
models.purpose: Score how much confidence CyberShield should place in the evidence supporting a decision or claim.

models.name: Confidence Score
models.purpose: Separate confidence from trust so a claim or decision can be trusted but low-confidence, or high-confidence but high-risk.

models.name: Internet Artifact Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate an online artifact as untrusted until verified, including the wrapper, source, transcript, claims, evidence chain, and sentence-level assertions.
models.pilot_scenario: USAFacts / Steve Ballmer video transcript pilot. The model asks which claims can be trusted, at what confidence, based on what evidence, and with what operational risk if believed.

models.name: Claim Extraction Model
models.purpose: Extract factual claims from a transcript, article, report, webpage, ad, or AI-generated artifact.

models.name: Fact Verification Model
models.purpose: Compare extracted claims against primary or trusted sources and classify whether each claim is supported, contradicted, incomplete, stale, or unverified.
models.boundary: No live fact-checking is performed in the current static prototype.

models.name: Source Credibility Model
models.purpose: Evaluate source authority, independence, reputation, proximity to primary evidence, and potential conflict of interest.

models.name: Risk-if-Wrong Model
models.purpose: Explain what could happen if CyberShield or the executive relies on the wrong assumption, claim, source, or evidence set.

models.name: Decision Consequence Model
models.purpose: Connect trust posture to approval, conditional approval, escalation, block, override, or risk acceptance.

models.name: Vendor Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate whether an external provider, platform, vendor, partner, or payment destination can be relied upon.

models.name: AI Governance Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate AI system or agent use based on provenance, authority, permitted use, tool access, evidence, and human review.

models.name: Human Verification Model
models.purpose: Track whether a qualified human has verified, approved, overridden, escalated, or accepted risk for a decision.

models.name: Proof Pack Artifact Model
models.purpose: Transform model outputs, evidence, assumptions, confidence, and human review into role-tailored defensible reports.
